response:
to solve this problem you must compute the sum of absolute differences for most indices while applying a square rule for every third index starting from zero. the key insight is that the array is sorted which allows us to use prefix sums to calculate the results in linear time without checking every pair. you can keep track of the total sum and the count of elements processed so far to derive the left and right contributions for each position k.

***
for indices where k is a multiple of three the calculation changes to the sum of squared differences which requires tracking the sum of squares as well as the sum of values. this approach ensures that the algorithm remains efficient even when the input size reaches one hundred thousand elements. by carefully updating the prefix sums and applying the correct formula based on the index you will generate the final result array that meets all the specified constraints.
